problemStatement: Agent Olympus has strong design-review coverage but suffers from three concrete gaps: (1) generic LLM UI defaults that even Aphrodite cannot prevent without explicit negative constraints and project-specific brand context; (2) always-on reviewer overhead in Atlas/Athena that wastes 60-80% of reviewer tokens on diffs irrelevant to most agents; (3) brownfield stalls where finish-branch assumes a test harness exists. Note: gap #3 (brownfield) is NOT solved in v1.0.2 — the actual test-bootstrap fix is deferred to v1.0.3. v1.0.2 ships partial mitigation only (browser pause + opt-in gates). A 3-way cross-review by Claude+Codex+Gemini against pbakaus/impeccable and garrytan/gstack reached unanimous consensus on five concrete adoptions plus four additional 2-of-3 consensus items. Revision 2 closes 11+ blockers identified by the consensus-plan and Momus reviews.
targetUsers: Solo AI developers running /atlas or /athena on UI-heavy projects; small teams wanting design-system discipline auto-enforced; brownfield rescuers (partial mitigation only — full fix in v1.0.3).
goals: Eliminate generic LLM UI defaults via opt-in anti-pattern registry as a finish-branch gate (warn-by-default in v1.0.2)

Make designer/aphrodite project-aware via /teach-design captured to .ao/memory/design-identity.json (NEW namespace, exempt from SessionEnd cleanup) and auto-injected through a refactored single-pass subagent-start hook with 2.5s wall-clock race
Replace monolithic design prompts with seven modular reference packs lazy-loaded by ui-review via scripts/lib/ui-reference.mjs (selectModules function with unit-test ACs)
Add precision style-pass micro-skills (/normalize, /polish, /typeset, /arrange) for surgical UI iteration — all with TDD
Cut Athena reviewer overhead 40-80% via regex-driven change-aware review router with reviewer-triggered escalation flag
Add browser pause + manual continue protocol (deterministic exact-resume deferred to v1.0.3)
Add stage artifact ARCHIVAL pipe (.ao/artifacts/pipe/<runId>/<stage>/) — NOT prompt-history isolation; isolation requires fresh-process architecture, out of scope
Add deterministic frontend remediation chain (audit -> normalize -> polish -> re-audit) with convergence check; NO harden stage in v1.0.2
Add taste memory (.ao/memory/taste.jsonl) for aesthetic preference accumulation distinct from wisdom
All existing tests continue to pass; SubagentStart hook p95 latency under 1500ms with 1MB synthetic fixtures
Standalone operation without impeccable or gstack installed
All new persisted formats carry schemaVersion: 1; loaders fail-safe on schemaVersion > known
nonGoals: Port impeccable's full 20-command surface (only 4 highest-leverage micro-skills)
Port gstack's /learn (wisdom covers it) or /autoplan (Atlas/Athena cover it)
Port gstack's GStack Browser binary or Pretext layout engine
Modify hook signatures or run.cjs entry point
Implement STRIDE security review or test-bootstrap (deferred to v1.0.3)
Change Greek mythology agent persona structure
Implement TTHW benchmarking — DEFERRED to v1.0.3 (does not solve brownfield stall, clone-source/privacy/cost contract undefined)
Implement deterministic browser exact-resume — DEFERRED to v1.0.3 (UA/TLS fingerprint persistence is its own design pass)
Implement strict prompt-history isolation between orchestrator stages — out of scope (continuous-session architecture incompatible)
Add a /harden 5th micro-skill — out of scope (existing security-reviewer + impeccable:audit + a11y-audit cover it; remediation chain ends with re-audit convergence instead)

foundationStories.title: SubagentStart hook refactor — single-pass loader with 2.5s wall-clock race (M-B2 fix)
foundationStories.rationale: PRE-REQUISITE for US-003 and US-009. The current sync 3s-budget hook already does 4 sequential queryWisdom() reads. Adding 2 more file reads will silently kill context injection on slow disks. This refactor MUST land before US-003/US-009 implementation.
foundationStories.asA: subagent being spawned (designer, aphrodite, code-reviewer, etc.)
foundationStories.iWantTo: receive wisdom + design-identity + taste context within 1500ms p95, never blocking longer than 2500ms wall-clock
foundationStories.soThat: context injection is reliable on slow disks and CI containers, and never silently degrades
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN scripts/lib/subagent-context.mjs exposes loadContextBundle({agentName, budgetMs}) WHEN called THEN it returns {wisdom, designIdentity, taste, metadata} from a single-pass concurrent file read (Promise.all) under the budget

GIVEN the hook body wraps loadContextBundle in Promise.race with a 2500ms wall-clock cap WHEN the budget is exceeded THEN the hook returns {} (empty additionalContext) and logs a debug warning to .ao/state/ao-subagent-latency.log (suppressOutput)
GIVEN scripts/subagent-start.mjs is refactored to call subagent-context.mjs WHEN run THEN it makes ONE wisdom read (not 4), with category split done in-memory after the read

GIVEN hooks/hooks.json SubagentStart entry WHEN inspected THEN it remains SYNC by default (matching the existing context-producing hook pattern in scripts/session-start.mjs); async:true is allowed ONLY after a compatibility test in scripts/test/subagent-start-async.test.mjs proves that Claude Code still delivers additionalContext from async SubagentStart hooks. The default is sync; async is opt-in and gated behind a passing compatibility test, both documented in the implementation PR description.

GIVEN a regression test fixture with synthetic 1MB wisdom.jsonl + 1MB taste.jsonl + 4KB design-identity.json WHEN scripts/test/subagent-start-latency.test.mjs runs THEN p95 latency is under 1500ms on the slowest CI runner
GIVEN any of the three loaders throws WHEN encountered THEN the bundle returns the partial result (other loaders still succeed) — fail-safe per loader
GIVEN autonomy.json contains {subagentContext: {disabled: true}} WHEN the hook runs THEN it short-circuits and returns {} immediately
filesTouched: hooks/hooks.json (only if async compatibility test passes — sync is the default)

foundationStories.title: .ao/memory/ namespace + SessionEnd exemption (B-X1 + AC-X2 fix)
foundationStories.rationale: PRE-REQUISITE for US-003 and US-009. Without this, the new 'memory' files would be deleted by SessionEnd after 24h of inactivity. Also fixes worktree divergence: .ao/memory/ paths resolve via git-common-dir, mirroring scripts/lib/wisdom.mjs and scripts/lib/session-registry.mjs.
foundationStories.asA: developer running Atlas/Athena across multiple worktrees
foundationStories.iWantTo: have durable memory files (design identity, taste) survive 24h of inactivity AND be shared across all worktrees of the same project
foundationStories.soThat: learning persists and Athena workers see the same memory as the host project
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN scripts/lib/memory.mjs exposes resolveMemoryDir() WHEN called THEN it returns the absolute path to .ao/memory/ resolved via git rev-parse --git-common-dir (or process.cwd() fallback), mirroring scripts/lib/wisdom.mjs:17
GIVEN scripts/lib/memory.mjs exposes readMemoryFile(name) and writeMemoryFile(name, data) WHEN called THEN they atomic-write/safe-read JSON or JSONL under resolveMemoryDir() with mode 0o600
GIVEN scripts/session-end.mjs cleanStaleFiles loop runs over .ao/state/ WHEN executed THEN it never touches .ao/memory/ (verified by an entry-skip allow-list AND an explicit unit test)

GIVEN scripts/test/session-end.test.mjs is extended WHEN run THEN it creates a fixture .ao/memory/design-identity.json with mtime > 24h ago AND verifies the file is NOT deleted after cleanStaleFiles runs
GIVEN an Athena worktree at .claude/worktrees/<slug>/ WHEN it imports memory.mjs and calls resolveMemoryDir() THEN it returns the SAME absolute path as the host project (not a worktree-local path)
GIVEN .ao/memory/ does not exist WHEN any loader is called THEN it returns the empty default ({} or []) without error and without creating the directory unless a writer is invoked
GIVEN autonomy.json contains {memory: {disabled: true}} WHEN any memory loader is called THEN it returns the empty default without touching disk
GIVEN a memory file with schemaVersion > 1 WHEN loaded THEN the loader returns the empty default and logs a clear error (suppressOutput) — fail-safe forward compat

userStories.title: Anti-pattern registry + opt-in UI smell scan with warn|block mode
userStories.iWantTo: have an OPT-IN automated scanner that flags canonical LLM UI slop (Inter/system fonts, pure black, card-nesting, bounce easing, gray-on-color)
userStories.soThat: my project never ships visually-default AI output, while existing finish-branch users are not silently broken
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN config/design-blacklist.jsonc DOES NOT exist WHEN finish-branch runs THEN ui-smell-scan is skipped silently (opt-in default)
GIVEN config/design-blacklist.jsonc.example is shipped in v1.0.2 WHEN inspected THEN it documents the 5 canonical patterns and the schemaVersion: 1 field

GIVEN config/design-blacklist.jsonc exists with schemaVersion: 1 + anti-pattern rules WHEN ui-smell-scan.mjs runs THEN it loads the rules and reports each detection with file:line, rule id, and matched substring
GIVEN a known-bad seed fixture with all 5 canonical slop patterns WHEN scan runs THEN it detects at least 4 of the 5 (raised from 3)
GIVEN a project sets allowedFonts in .ao/memory/design-identity.json WHEN scan encounters that font THEN it does not flag

GIVEN autonomy.json contains {uiSmellScan: 'warn'} (the v1.0.2 default) AND finish-branch runs WHEN ui-smell-scan finds violations THEN finish-branch logs the violations as warnings and CONTINUES (does not fail)

GIVEN autonomy.json contains {uiSmellScan: 'block'} (opt-in stricter mode) AND finish-branch runs WHEN ui-smell-scan finds violations THEN finish-branch fails with structured error listing all violations
GIVEN scan finds zero violations WHEN finish-branch continues THEN it logs the clean result to .ao/artifacts/runs/<runId>/ui-smell-scan.json (with schemaVersion: 1)
GIVEN a project has no UI files in the diff (no .css/.scss/.tsx/.jsx/.vue/.svelte/.html) WHEN finish-branch runs THEN ui-smell-scan is skipped silently regardless of mode

userStories.title: Modular design reference pack (7 modules) + ui-reference.mjs execution surface
userStories.asA: designer or aphrodite agent reviewing UI
userStories.iWantTo: load only the relevant design reference module (typography, color-and-contrast, spatial-design, motion-design, interaction-design, responsive-design, ux-writing) for the current critique via a programmatic selectModules() function
userStories.soThat: my context window is not bloated by all 7 domains when only 1-2 apply, AND the module-selection logic is unit-testable (M-B1 fix)
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN scripts/lib/ui-reference.mjs exposes selectModules({diffPaths: string[], diffContent?: string}) WHEN called THEN it returns a string[] of module names from the canonical 7-module set
GIVEN selectModules is called with diffPaths=['src/styles/buttons.css'] WHEN run THEN it returns ['color-and-contrast','spatial-design','responsive-design']

GIVEN selectModules is called with diffPaths=['src/components/Button.tsx'] AND diffContent contains className/style/font WHEN run THEN it returns at minimum ['typography','color-and-contrast','spatial-design']
GIVEN selectModules is called with diffPaths=['src/i18n/errors.json'] OR copy-only changes (label/error message diffs) WHEN run THEN it returns ['ux-writing']
GIVEN selectModules is called with diffPaths containing keyframes/transition/animation patterns WHEN run THEN it returns ['motion-design','interaction-design']
GIVEN selectModules is called with diffPaths that match no rule WHEN run THEN it returns the full 7-module set AND a warning entry (logged via debug)
GIVEN skills/ui-review/reference/ contains 7 markdown files WHEN ui-review skill runs THEN it calls selectModules() with the diff paths and loads only the returned modules
GIVEN each reference module WHEN inspected THEN it contains: domain definition, top 5 principles, top 5 anti-patterns, 1 worked example, schemaVersion frontmatter
GIVEN scripts/test/ui-reference.test.mjs WHEN run THEN it has at least 7 test cases covering each of: css-only, tsx+style, copy-only, motion, no-match-fallback, mixed-bag, empty-input

userStories.title: /teach-design identity briefing + auto-injection via subagent-context loader
userStories.asA: developer setting up Olympus on a new frontend project
userStories.iWantTo: run /teach-design once to capture brand colors, typography tokens, spacing scale, component library, and design conventions into .ao/memory/design-identity.json
userStories.soThat: every subsequent designer/aphrodite/ui-review invocation references my project-specific taste rather than re-deriving generic 'good design'
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN /teach-design is invoked WHEN the skill runs THEN it interviews the user (or auto-detects from package.json/tailwind.config/theme files) to populate brand colors, fonts, spacing tokens, allowedFonts, and design conventions

GIVEN /teach-design completes WHEN saved THEN .ao/memory/design-identity.json (NOT .ao/state/) contains schema-validated fields: schemaVersion: 1, brand.colors, typography.fonts, spacing.scale, components.library, allowedFonts, conventions.notes
GIVEN scripts/lib/design-identity.mjs exposes loadIdentity() WHEN called THEN it reads via memory.mjs (worktree-shared) and returns the parsed object or {} on error

GIVEN .ao/memory/design-identity.json exists AND is over 2KB WHEN loadIdentity is called THEN it returns a summarized projection (top-level keys only + first 3 colors + first 2 fonts) capped at 2KB to enforce the context budget
GIVEN subagent-context.mjs.loadContextBundle() runs for designer/aphrodite/ui-review subagent type WHEN called THEN it injects the (capped) identity as a section of additionalContext
GIVEN subagent without design-identity.json WHEN loadContextBundle runs THEN it injects nothing extra (no error, fail-safe per F-001)

GIVEN identity file is updated WHEN /teach-design re-runs THEN existing fields are merged with last-write-wins per top-level key (documented merge semantics: deep-merge for objects, replace for arrays/scalars); user is shown the diff before save
GIVEN identity is corrupted WHEN loadIdentity tries to load it THEN function returns {} (fail-safe, never blocks)
GIVEN identity file has schemaVersion > 1 WHEN loadIdentity runs THEN it returns {} and the hook logs a clear error (suppressOutput)

userStories.title: Precision style-pass micro-skills (/normalize, /polish, /typeset, /arrange) — TDD
userStories.asA: developer iterating on a UI component
userStories.iWantTo: invoke a surgical micro-skill that fixes one specific concern (token alignment, final polish, typography only, layout/spacing only) without re-generating the whole component
userStories.soThat: I can iterate in 10% shifts instead of full redesigns, preserving layout stability
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN /normalize is invoked on a target file or component WHEN it runs THEN it replaces hardcoded values with design tokens, leaves layout untouched, reports a diff summary
GIVEN /polish is invoked WHEN it runs THEN it applies final-pass refinements (alignment, spacing rhythm, micro-detail) without changing structure or copy
GIVEN /typeset is invoked WHEN it runs THEN it adjusts font choices, hierarchy, sizing, weight only — touches no other CSS
GIVEN /arrange is invoked WHEN it runs THEN it adjusts layout, spacing, visual rhythm only — touches no typography or color
GIVEN any micro-skill completes WHEN it finishes THEN it reports: files touched, lines changed, scope confirmation (e.g. '0 typography changes' for /arrange)
GIVEN any micro-skill is invoked WHEN it routes THEN it uses the designer agent at sonnet tier (not opus) for cost efficiency

GIVEN scripts/test/micro-skills-scope.test.mjs runs WHEN executed THEN it asserts each micro-skill's scope-confirmation contract on a fixture diff (e.g. /arrange MUST report 0 typography changes when given a CSS-only typography-free diff)
GIVEN /arrange runs on a diff that contains font-related changes WHEN scope check fails THEN the skill reports a scope violation and exits 1

userStories.title: Regex-driven change-aware review router with reviewer-triggered escalation
userStories.asA: Atlas or Athena orchestrator about to spawn reviewers
userStories.iWantTo: consult a router that maps the current diff scope to the minimal set of relevant reviewer agents AND lets any reviewer mid-run pull security-reviewer back in via an escalation flag
userStories.soThat: I do not waste 60-80% of reviewer tokens running architect/security on a CSS-only diff or aphrodite on a backend refactor, while still catching security-relevant code in shared utilities
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN config/review-routing.jsonc contains schemaVersion: 1 + diff-scope rules (frontend/backend/infra/docs/test/auth) WHEN review-router.mjs is called with a diff THEN it returns the list of reviewer agents to invoke

GIVEN review-routing.jsonc contains a securityPatterns regex set WHEN inspected THEN the set MUST include at minimum: token, secret, apikey, api[_-]?key, credential, hmac, signature, sign(ed|ing)?, bcrypt, argon, scrypt, pbkdf, privatekey, private[_-]?key, rsa, ecdsa, csrf, xsrf, saml, oidc, jwks, kms, vault, jwt, session, password, oauth, cookie, refresh.?token, Authorization, bearer, client[_-]?secret, access[_-]?key, secret[_-]?access[_-]?key, ssh, OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY, ssh-(rsa|ed25519|dss|ecdsa), connection.?string, database.?url, db[_-]?url, \.pem, \.p12, \.keystore, \.env

GIVEN patterns are stored in the config as regex strings WHEN review-router compiles them THEN they are used as RegExp instances (not String.includes), so 'apikey' matches 'apikey' AND 'api_key' AND 'api-key' AND 'apiKey' via the api[_-]?key pattern
GIVEN a CSS-only diff WHEN router is called THEN it returns [aphrodite, designer] (not architect/security/code-reviewer)
GIVEN a backend-only diff WHEN router is called THEN it returns [code-reviewer, architect, security-reviewer] (not aphrodite/designer)
GIVEN a diff matching ANY securityPatterns regex WHEN router is called THEN it always includes security-reviewer regardless of other rules

GIVEN a chaos-test fixture with 30+ obfuscated secret patterns (jwt-decode, .env.local, signed-url, crypto.subtle, refresh_token, RSA private key blocks, etc.) WHEN router runs THEN security-reviewer is included for ALL 30+ cases (zero false negatives)

GIVEN a reviewer (e.g. code-reviewer) detects security-relevant code in a shared utility/config and emits a structured escalation flag {type: 'RE-REVIEW-REQUESTED', additionalReviewer: 'security-reviewer', reason: '...'} WHEN the orchestrator receives the flag mid-run THEN it spawns the requested reviewer in the same iteration (not the next loop)
GIVEN routing.alwaysInclude contains an agent WHEN router is called THEN that agent is always included
GIVEN router has no rule match WHEN called THEN it falls back to the full reviewer set with a warning logged
GIVEN Atlas Phase 4 verification runs WHEN router is consulted THEN reviewer fan-out uses only the returned set
GIVEN Athena worker integration runs WHEN router is consulted THEN cross-validation uses only the returned set
GIVEN autonomy.json contains {reviewRouter: {disabled: true}} WHEN router is called THEN it returns the full reviewer set immediately (rollback path)

userStories.title: Browser pause + manual continue protocol (deterministic exact-resume DEFERRED to v1.0.3)
userStories.asA: developer running autonomous browser-based QA via Atlas/Athena
userStories.iWantTo: have the agent pause when it hits CAPTCHA/auth/MFA, log the URL + sanitized breadcrumb, and notify me to complete the step manually in my own browser
userStories.soThat: self-driving QA does not stall on real-world auth gates, while remaining honest about the lack of deterministic resume
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN agent encounters a recognized auth/captcha pattern (DOM markers: recaptcha, hcaptcha, 'verify you are human', login form) WHEN handoff is triggered THEN it persists session ID + sanitized current URL + sanitized breadcrumb to .ao/state/browser-handoff.json (schemaVersion: 1) and emits a notification

GIVEN URL persistence WHEN inspected THEN query string is sanitized to strip access_token, id_token, code, state, sig, signature, secret, key, password, auth, session, token, jwt, hmac, otp, recovery, refresh — patterns matched as regex against parameter names

GIVEN breadcrumb persistence WHEN inspected THEN it contains ONLY allow-listed fields: {step: string, lastClickedSelector: string, screenshotPath?: string}. Everything else (form values, localStorage, headers, cookies, fetch payloads) is FORBIDDEN by an explicit deny-list test
GIVEN handoff state is older than 24h WHEN read THEN it is treated as stale; user must restart the QA flow
GIVEN no browser provider available WHEN handoff is triggered THEN it logs the URL and instructs user to manually complete; never blocks indefinitely

GIVEN the user completes the auth step manually WHEN they invoke /resume-handoff THEN the orchestrator displays the persisted breadcrumb and asks the user to confirm the new starting state — it does NOT attempt deterministic auto-resume in v1.0.2

GIVEN US-006 ships WHEN inspected THEN skills/resume-handoff/SKILL.md exists as a thin state-reader skill: (a) reads .ao/state/browser-handoff.json, (b) prints persisted URL + breadcrumb, (c) prompts user for confirmation token, (d) records resume event in run artifact. NO browser-session abstraction is required at runtime.
GIVEN handoff/resume succeeds WHEN orchestrator continues THEN the run artifact records both the pause and resume events with timestamps
GIVEN spec.md WHEN inspected THEN it explicitly notes that deterministic exact-resume (UA/TLS fingerprint persistence) is DEFERRED to v1.0.3
GIVEN autonomy.json contains {browserHandoff: {disabled: true}} WHEN auth pattern is detected THEN handoff is skipped and the orchestrator logs a warning
filesTouched: skills/resume-handoff/SKILL.md (new — thin state-reader, no browser-session abstraction)

userStories.title: Cascade artifact ARCHIVAL pipe (NOT isolation) + CLAUDE.md State Management doc update
userStories.asA: Atlas or Athena orchestrator running a multi-stage workflow
userStories.iWantTo: write each stage's outputs to .ao/artifacts/pipe/<runId>/<stage>/outbox/ as a postmortem-friendly archive, and have the next stage receive a structured handoff manifest
userStories.soThat: stage outputs are auditable and the next stage can reference prior outputs without dumping full conversation history into the prompt — this is ARCHIVAL, not strict prompt-history isolation
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN spec.md and this prd.json WHEN inspected THEN US-007 explicitly disclaims that this is ARCHIVAL ONLY: continuous-session orchestrators cannot deliver strict prompt-history isolation; isolation requires fresh-process stage runners which is out of scope for v1.0.2

GIVEN scripts/lib/artifact-pipe.mjs exposes writeOutbox(runId, stage, name, payload) and readInbox(runId, stage) WHEN orchestrator stages call these THEN files are written to .ao/artifacts/pipe/<runId>/<stage>/{inbox,outbox}/ with schemaVersion: 1 in each payload

GIVEN canonical stage names WHEN documented THEN the allowed set is exactly: plan, decompose, execute, verify, review, finish (no free-form names — collisions are prevented by schema validation in writeOutbox)

GIVEN stage N completes WHEN stage N+1 starts THEN the orchestrator passes the inbox path explicitly in the next subagent prompt (a new manifest, not symlinks) so the next agent reads via tool call rather than via filesystem race

GIVEN a stage produces too much data WHEN written THEN payloads are capped at 100KB per file with a tail-truncation warning AND the per-run total is capped at 10MB (additional writes beyond cap are dropped with a logged warning)
GIVEN US-007 archival writes are in-process async (no subagent spawn per stage) WHEN ConcurrencyGate is consulted THEN it sees ZERO additional Task spawns from US-007 (no fan-out)

GIVEN a run completes successfully WHEN cleanup runs THEN .ao/artifacts/pipe/<runId>/ is preserved for 24h then deleted by SessionEnd (.ao/artifacts/pipe is OUTSIDE .ao/memory/, so SessionEnd CAN sweep it)
GIVEN a stage fails WHEN debugging THEN .ao/artifacts/pipe/<runId>/ is human-readable for postmortem

GIVEN US-007 ships WHEN inspected THEN CLAUDE.md State Management section is updated to enumerate .ao/memory/, .ao/artifacts/pipe/, and the new schemaVersion convention. This doc update is part of US-007 acceptance, not a separate story.
GIVEN concurrent writes from two stages WHEN they target the same outbox dir THEN atomic-write semantics from fs-atomic.mjs prevent partial-write corruption

userStories.title: Sequential frontend remediation chain (audit -> normalize -> polish -> re-audit convergence)
userStories.asA: developer who just received a ui-review report
userStories.iWantTo: invoke /ui-remediate to run a fixed audit -> normalize -> polish -> re-audit pipeline with each stage's result recorded and convergence verified
userStories.soThat: frontend fixes are deterministic, auditable, and the chain cannot oscillate (polish introducing a new typeset smell) without being detected
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN /ui-remediate is invoked on a target WHEN it runs THEN it executes audit (ui-review), normalize, polish, re-audit in strict order — NO harden stage (out of scope v1.0.2)
GIVEN any stage fails WHEN detected THEN the chain halts, records the failure to .ao/artifacts/runs/<runId>/ui-remediation.json, does not advance
GIVEN each stage completes WHEN the next starts THEN it receives only the prior stage's structured outbox payload (not full conversation history)

GIVEN the final re-audit stage runs WHEN it completes THEN it compares smell counts vs the initial audit and: (a) ABORTS with a regression warning if smell count INCREASED, (b) WARNS if smell count is unchanged, (c) succeeds if smell count DECREASED
GIVEN re-audit convergence check WHEN ui-remediation.json is written THEN it contains: initialSmellCount, finalSmellCount, delta, status (improved|unchanged|regressed)

GIVEN max-iterations cap WHEN /ui-remediate runs THEN it executes the chain exactly ONCE (no retry loop) — re-audit is a verification gate, not a retry trigger; users must re-invoke /ui-remediate manually
GIVEN the chain finishes WHEN it writes results THEN .ao/artifacts/runs/<runId>/ui-remediation.json contains schemaVersion: 1, stage, status, files touched, time elapsed, summary for each step
GIVEN the chain is invoked from finish-branch WHEN integrated THEN finish-branch waits for chain completion before continuing
GIVEN the chain depends on US-004 micro-skills (/normalize, /polish) WHEN they are missing THEN /ui-remediate fails fast with a clear error

GIVEN US-008 implementation WHEN it integrates with finish-branch THEN it does NOT spawn parallel subagents (sequential by design — single active worker per stage); ConcurrencyGate sees only 1 spawn at a time

userStories.title: Taste memory in .ao/memory/taste.jsonl with explicit /taste prune grammar
userStories.asA: developer who gives feedback like 'I prefer minimal layouts' or 'no rounded corners'
userStories.iWantTo: have those preferences accumulated in .ao/memory/taste.jsonl and replayed to designer/aphrodite via the subagent-context loader
userStories.soThat: my UI agent learns my taste over time without me re-stating it every session
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN scripts/lib/taste-memory.mjs exposes recordTaste(entry) and loadTaste(limit) WHEN called THEN entries are appended to .ao/memory/taste.jsonl (NOT .ao/state/) in JSONL format via memory.mjs

GIVEN a taste entry WHEN written THEN it contains: schemaVersion: 1, id (uuid), timestamp, source (user|auto), category (typography|color|layout|motion|copy), preference (string), antiPreference (string?), confidence (low|med|high)

GIVEN designer/aphrodite/ui-review subagent fires WHEN subagent-context.mjs.loadContextBundle runs THEN it reads loadTaste(20) and includes the most recent 20 entries as a section of additionalContext, capped at 1KB total
GIVEN taste.jsonl exceeds 200 entries WHEN appended THEN oldest entries are pruned (FIFO) to stay under cap

GIVEN HR-X3 fix: the user runs the taste skill (either dedicated /taste or the extended /sessions surface — implementer's choice, documented in spec.md) WHEN they request 'taste prune' with --id, --category, or --before THEN matching entries are removed
GIVEN the chosen surface WHEN US-009 ships THEN skills/taste/SKILL.md OR skills/sessions/SKILL.md (whichever is chosen) explicitly documents the 'taste prune' grammar — not implicit
GIVEN taste-memory file is corrupted WHEN loaded THEN function returns [] (fail-safe, never blocks)
GIVEN taste.jsonl entries with schemaVersion > 1 WHEN loaded THEN they are skipped (forward compat, fail-safe)
GIVEN wisdom and taste are siblings WHEN inspected THEN they live in separate files; wisdom records facts, taste records aesthetics; both share the same git-common-dir worktree resolution
filesTouched: skills/taste/SKILL.md (new) OR skills/sessions/SKILL.md (modify) — implementer's choice, documented

deferredStories.title: TTHW (Time To Hello World) benchmarking
deferredStories.deferredReason: Does NOT solve the brownfield-stall problem stated in spec.md (which requires test-bootstrap, not benchmarking). Clone-source/privacy/cost/network contract is undefined: per-run fresh git clone leaks branch existence, hits 5000 req/hr API limits on private repos, bypasses local uncommitted state, cannot work offline. Three reviewers (Codex B-X4, Gemini HR-G1, Momus M-H2) flagged this. Per pre-decided revision policy: when reviewers split on FIX vs DEFER, take the conservative low-risk path. Restoring scale-M ceiling (9 stories, was 10). The actual brownfield-stall fix is the v1.0.3 test-bootstrap story.
deferredStories.v103DesignNotes: Replace per-run fresh-clone with cached worktree at ~/.cache/agent-olympus/tthw/<repo-hash>; add --offline-skip flag; --depth=1 default; daily rate-limit cap; explicit clone-source param; ALSO add a separate test-bootstrap skill for the actual brownfield fix

compatibilityAndRollback.schemaVersioning: Every new persisted file format carries schemaVersion: 1. JSON files carry it as a top-level field; JSONL files carry it per-line. Loaders refuse schemaVersion > known with a clear error (suppressOutput) and continue with empty data (fail-safe).

ui-smell-scan.optOut: delete config/design-blacklist.jsonc OR set autonomy.json.uiSmellScan = 'warn' (already default) OR delete the project's UI files

subagent-context.rollback: hook fail-opens to empty additionalContext on any error

.ao/memory/.optOut: autonomy.json.memory.disabled = true (all loaders return empty defaults)
.ao/memory/.rollback: delete .ao/memory/ directory; no other state touches it

review-router.default: always on with full-set fallback if no rule match

compatibilityAndRollback.autonomyJsonBackwardCompat: ALL new fields are optional. Loaders default to current behavior when fields are absent. The autonomy.json schema is NOT broken.

phase2_parallel: B: US-003 + US-005 + US-009 (US-003 and US-009 share infra and MUST be one delivery)
executionOrder.concurrencyGateNote: Cap is 5 global, 3 Claude. Phase 2 + Phase 4 cannot all run truly in parallel; orchestrator must batch.

revisionHistory.summary: Initial draft. NEEDS_REVISION (consensus-plan), NO_GO (Momus). 11+ blockers identified.

11+ blockers resolved per .ao/work-order-negotiated.md. Highlights: F-001 (M-B2 SubagentStart refactor), F-002 (.ao/memory/ namespace + B-X1 fix + AC-X2 worktree contract), US-001 opt-in default + warn|block mode (M-B3), US-002 ui-reference.mjs execution surface (M-B1), US-005 regex security router + escalation (P-B1), US-006 downgrade to pause-only (HR-G2), US-007 reframe to archival (B-X2 + D6), US-008 drop harden + add re-audit convergence (B-G1 + AC-G2 + D7), US-010 DEFER (B-X4), all new schemas carry schemaVersion: 1 (M-B3).
